title: Does the verilator support the conversion of 3D arrays (4D arrays counting the bit width)?
body:
Hello, does the verilator support the conversion of 3D arrays (4D arrays counting the bit width)?
During the verification, the function of the converted C++ code is inconsistent with that of the original RTL code.

```
reg  [1:0][7:0][7:0][15:0] buffer0;
reg  [7:0][15:0][15:0] buffer1;
reg  x;

always a* begin
    for (int i=0;i<8;i++) begin
        for (int j=0;j<16;j++) begin
            if (j<8 ) begin
                buffer1[i][j] = buffer0[x][i][j];
            end else begin
                buffer1[i][j] = buffer0[~x][i][j-8];
            end
        end
    end
end
```

Is the above writing supported?
I would be very grateful if you could answer me.
